title: Opening keynote
speakers: Dylan Field (CEO & Co-founder, Figma)
abstract: Design and product-building are changing, and so are the roles we play in the process. This means rethinking product development from the ground up—shifting mindsets and methods to make great work. Figma’s biggest product launch of the year kicks off Config.

title: Keynote
speakers: Holly Herndon (Artist, Herndon Dryhurst Studio)

title: Figma learning lab: design to development workflows
speakers: Riccardo Erra (Developer Advocate, Figma)
abstract: A beginner-friendly guide to moving from design to code using Figma’s MCP server. Learn best practices for bringing code context into Figma with variable prefixes and Code Connect, and how to get stronger results from your design system. Join us afterward in the Learning Lab Lounge for informal time with the Figma team.

title: Welcome and opening remarks
speakers: Sheila Vashee (Chief Marketing Officer, Figma), Noah Levin (VP, Design, Figma)

title: Scooter, screen and duct-tape: from hack to habit
speakers: Unni Krishnan Manikoth (Digital Experience Lead, Ather Energy ), Harish Kumar (Product Designer, Ather Energy )
abstract: What if your next great design tool doesn’t exist yet? At Ather Energy, designers built their own using Arduinos, iPads, and Figma. This talk unpacks the ways of going outside the design loop, how hacks become habits, and how scrappy experimentation helps teams validate ideas, influence stakeholders, and build magical real-world experiences faster.

title: Get animated with Figma Motion
speakers: Daniela Muntyan (Product Designer, Figma), Luca Damasco (Manager, Software Engineering, Figma), Dave Hornsby (Product Manager, Figma)

title: From kickoff to canvas: connecting Google Workspace and Figma
speakers: Addison Rodomista (UX Design Lead, Google ), Chloe Fagan-Tucker (Google Workspace, BD & Strategic Partnerships, Google)

title: Ready for the world
speakers: Vicki Tan (Designer and Author)

title: Trust as a through line
speakers: Thomas Vidal (VP Product Design , Accor), Deidre Kolarick (Managing Vice President, Head of Design for Bank, Business, and Card, Capital One), Katie Szeto (Manager, Product Management, Figma)

title: Scaling taste, craft, and quality in the AI era
speakers: Teo Connor (VP Design, Airbnb), Loredana Crisan (Chief Design Officer, Figma)

title: Beyond design systems: designing for robots and seniors
speakers: Anna Oh (Head of Product and Design, Norbert Health)

title: Dimensional shift: sculpting in Figma Draw
speakers: Josh McKenna (Independent Illustrator)
abstract: What happens when a successful career hits a wall? Illustrator Josh McKenna shares his personal journey through creative block and how learning a new program saved his practice. In this session, Josh demonstrates his unified Figma Draw workflow, sharing practical techniques for using 2D vector effects to create 3D depth. Discover how becoming a student again makes your brain 'porous' to new dimensions, bridging the gap between flat design and 3D. Learn why picking up a new tool isn't just a technical upgrade, it’s a creative lifeline.

title: Figma meets hardware: prototyping for the physical world
speakers: Frederik Ueberschär (Senior Designer for Creative Technology, Electrolux), Sam Thorne (Senior Principal Designer for Creative Technology, Electrolux)
abstract: What if Figma’s canvas could extend into the physical world? At Electrolux, we use Figma to design the next generation of home appliances. This talk shares how we bridged the digital-physical gap by building "Figma Displays" with off-the-shelf electronics to live-stream prototypes onto production-accurate screens. Learn how this new process shrunk our feedback loop from six months to just two minutes, completely revamping our workflow. You'll leave inspired to find your own ways to connect the digital canvas to the world around you.

title: How to build content systems for AI product workflows
speakers: Jessica Ouyang (Co-founder, Ditto), Jolena Ma (Co-founder, Ditto)

title: Out of sync, on purpose: desktop design after mobile won
speakers: Giulio Fagiolini (Staff Designer, Spotify)
abstract: This talk is about designing for Spotify’s desktop app—the original Spotify experience that continues to evolve alongside mobile. It explores how a small, focused team interprets features not by copying them 1:1, but by tailoring execution to the platform’s strengths. Desktop plays a distinct role in people’s routines—a companion for deep focus, creation, and immersive listening. Through real examples, Giulio shows how being small, deliberate, and a little out of sync can transform consistency into craft.

title: How to build an AI-native org
speakers: Anique Drumright (Chief Product Officer, Harvey), Christina Nguyen White (VP, Design, Harvey), Gui Seiz (Design, Director, Figma)
abstract: Join the Harvey team for a facilitated roundtable discussion on how AI is reshaping product development and blurring the boundaries between design and product. In small-group conversations, participants will openly discuss evolving org structures, changing hiring dynamics, and what it means to build in a world where anyone can create.

title: Figma learning lab: the many ways to make with Figma Make
speakers: Alexia Danton (Designer Advocate, Figma), Brett McMillin (Designer Advocate, Figma)
abstract: Learn about all of the different ways to build with Figma Make, whether you’re creating a new prototype from scratch or starting from an existing codebase. We’ll cover guidelines & skills, how to provide great context, and some best practices for working with your design system. Join us afterward in the Learning Lab Lounge for informal time with the Figma team.

title: Writing for humans in an AI world
speakers: Chelsea Larsson (Content Design, Anthropic)
abstract: Content designers at Anthropic don't write Claude's responses—they write everything around them: prompt starters, UI copy, feature names, voice principles. This talk explores how content design shifts when your interface understands natural language and how to teach collaboration (not just use) through the edges of the experience. Whether you're designing AI products or wondering how AI changes the craft, this talk addresses the challenge of designing frames around intelligence that talks back.

title: Be the director: how stage craft informs product craft
speakers: Kim Beverett (Product Manager, OpenAI)
abstract: The screen is the stage, and the user is the audience. In this talk, we’ll explore how to think like a stage director by making intentional choices, defining the non-negotiables in your script, and finding your product’s spine. And why the show must go on.

title: From concept to confidence with Dscout AI
speakers: Lauren Madura (Director of Product Design, Dscout), Michael Winnick (CEO & founder, Dscout)

title: Skill issue: how we built an OS for PMs at Figma
speakers: Jinen Kamdar (Product Lead, Figma), Lawrence Luk (Product Technical Program Manager, Figma)

title: Designing the product that can do anything
speakers: Brian Ringley (Distinguished Product Manager & HRI Designer, Boston Dynamics)
abstract: Software, robotics, machine intelligence, and spatial computing have collided to present us with one of the greatest UX challenges of our time: How do you design an interface for a humanoid robot that can theoretically do anything? This talk will dive into the design challenges of teaching a robot about the world in three parts: Training robots to develop skills, building application interfaces to author task behaviors, and integrating with facilities to monitor robots’ effectiveness and realize the vision of the software-defined factory.

title: Navigating the age of agentic computing
speakers: Hector Ouilhet Olmos (VP of Design, AWS Applied AI Solutions, Amazon), Shani Taylor (VP, Customer Experience & Solutions, Figma)

title: Trust, transparency, and flow: the UX of Gemini Enterprise
speakers: Sheta Chatterjee (Senior Director, Head of UX, Cloud AI, Google), Yuhki Yamashita (Chief Product Officer, Figma)

title: Figma learning lab: bringing designs to life with Figma Motion
speakers: Miggi (Designer Advocate, Education, Figma)
abstract: Ease into motion in this session; we’ll show how to bring more energy into your product experience. Everything you need to know to get started with these new features. Join us afterward in the Learning Lab Lounge for informal time with the Figma team.

title: Figma deep dive: designing with the Figma agent
speakers: Rodrigo Davies (Product Lead, Agents, Figma)

title: Prompts to fashion: AI-powered 3D-printed garments
speakers: Danit Peleg (Founder, 3D-Printed Fashion Lab )
abstract: Danit Peleg's vision is a new paradigm for fashion production: an AI-powered workflow that converts sewing patterns into full-size soft 3D-printable textiles- prompts to 3D-printed fashion, using bio-based, zero-microplastic materials developed for wearable, soft textiles. This session brings that vision to the Config stage through a live reveal of 3 looks designed with Weavy and printed using this new workflow, showing the design community what zero-waste, on-demand, fully digitized fashion actually looks and feels like. Danit is making clear that the tools to build a circular, on-demand fashion future already exist today.

title: Design leadership in the age of acceleration
speakers: Jen Dunnam (VP, Design, (Formerly) Patreon), Pedro Hernandez (Advocacy Manager, Figma)
abstract: As product development accelerates and expectations around craft continue to rise, design leaders are navigating a constant tension: moving fast without losing clarity, quality, or creative foundations. In this conversation, SoundCloud VP of Design Will Mazuel and Jen Dunnam, VP Design, Patreon, discuss what it takes to build resilient design organizations today—from partnering effectively with product and engineering leadership to creating systems, rituals, and principles that hold up under pressure. Drawing from their experience supporting creators, they'll also explore how thoughtful processes and systems can enable healthy, resilient, and lasting communities.

title: Applied Insights: the state of AI and design (Option 1)
speakers: Andrew Hogan (Head of Insights, Figma), Shane Johnston (Researcher, Figma), Christa Simon (Manager, Research, Figma)
abstract: In this session, offered on both days, the Figma research team will share key findings from our newly-released 2026 AI report. Together, we’ll unpack three years of AI trends: where impact is accelerating, how investment is shifting, and what's actually changing about how teams work together. Participants will have the opportunity to react, compare experiences, and pressure-test what’s actually sticking in practice.

title: Hacking AI for creative operations at scale
speakers: Rory Flynn (Founder, Systematiq Ai)

title: Chaos as a catalyst: building Sephora’s next operating model
speakers: Sneha Narahalli (SVP, Technology, Sephora), Paige Costello (VP Product, Figma)
abstract: Sephora is on a journey to become an AI-native organization—but the shift goes far beyond features. It requires working through ambiguity and writing the playbook in real time. In this fireside chat, we’ll explore how teams are building new ways of working, defining patterns as they go, and shaping a scalable playbook—while continuing to deliver inclusive, intuitive, and brand-forward experiences.

title: Figma learning lab: unlocking speed and visual expression with generative plugins and shader effects
speakers: Hugo Raymond (Designer Advocate, Figma), Mallory Dean (Designer Advocate, Figma)
abstract: Learn about new ways to bring visual expression, consistency, and speed into your design workflow with generative effects and shader effects. Join us afterward in the Learning Lab Lounge for informal time with the Figma team.

title: Generative plugins: building plugins has never been easier
speakers: Avery Frankenberg (Software Engineer, Figma), Jason Calleiro (Product Designer, Figma)

title: The most important component: community
speakers: Alicia Kranjc (Figma Community Product Manager, Figma)

title: From handoffs to upstream
speakers: Frederick Andersen (Founder, EDL )
abstract: A year ago, EDL made a bet. Not on a tool or a trend, but on a person and a different idea of what a design studio could own. Today, EDL pushes to clients' Git repos, runs their design systems in code, and sits upstream of their engineering teams. This wasn't accidental. It was intentional. This is the full story: the moment the team saw the opportunity, the rituals that made it real, the metrics that proved it worked, and what it means for any design leader wondering if their team could do the same.

title: From idea to production: bridging design systems and code with AI
speakers: Abdullah Alkhatib (Principal Designer, Mercari, Inc.), Motoki Narita (VP of Product Engineering (Marketplace), Mercari, Inc.), Marcel Weekes (VP, Product Engineering, Figma)
abstract: The gap between design and production code is rapidly shrinking. AI can turn ideas, sketches, and screenshots into UI—but without design systems, they remain prototypes. This talk explores how connecting AI-generated output to system components enables real, production-ready code. The Mercari team will share workflows and lessons they’ve learned to double development speed while improving quality.

title: Designing OpenAI’s products: Rethinking design in the age of AI
speakers: Ian Silber (Head of Product Design, Open AI), Noah Levin (VP, Design, Figma)

title: How structured thinking gives your AI superpowers
speakers: Carola Pescio Canale (Head of Design, AI, Atlassian)

title: The metacognitive design loop
speakers: Jenny Au (Team Lead, UX QA Engineering, Epsilon/Publicis Groupe), Mike Green (Team Lead, Product Design, Epsilon/Publicis Groupe)
abstract: Learn how Epsilon's Product Design Team transformed front-end development by combining Figma's Code Connect and MCP with generative AI layout creation. Using their CORE UI components, the team has revolutionized product delivery and achieved unprecedented development velocity—moving from design to production faster than ever before.

title: Human methods, new material: designing with AI for the classroom
speakers: Mubarak "Moby" Marafa (Staff Product Designer, Goodnotes), Wendy Liao (Learning and Development Specialist, Goodnotes)
abstract: How do you build with AI for a world as human, messy, and high-stakes as the classroom? This talk takes you inside the 0–1 journey of Goodnotes Classroom: from real teacher workarounds and classroom observations to fast prototypes, co-creation sessions, and product decisions shaped by educators themselves. We’ll share how human methods became the foundation for designing with AI as a new material, and why the best way to build faster may be to get closer to the people you’re building for.

title: Shaders in Figma: create the effects and fills you've always wanted
speakers: Yassir Solomah (Software Engineering Manager, Figma), Piers Cowburn (Director of Engineering, Expressive Design, Figma), Rogie King (Product Designer, Figma)

title: The edge case is everyone
speakers: Willie King (Business Development Manager, T-Mobile ), Lex Liley (Associate Director, Product Strategy, Kettle)
abstract: Most communication technology was built for an able-bodied world. For more than 11 million Americans who are Deaf or Hard of Hearing, a phone call can still be a barrier. T-Mobile set out to rethink that experience. Willie King, Business Development Manager at T-Mobile, and Lex Liley, Associate Director of Product Strategy at Kettle, will share how their teams partnered with the DHH community to challenge assumptions about voice communication. A look at inclusive design and why solving hard challenges leads to better experiences for everyone.

title: Your best practices are trapped in your head—and your AI has no idea
speakers: Harald Kirschner (Principal Product Manager, Microsoft)
abstract: Your tone of voice, your component principles, your definition of "done"—this is the taste you've built over years. Right now it lives in your head, in scattered docs, or in places your tools can't see. Every AI session starts from scratch because that context is invisible. This talk shows how to connect those dots: building a shared knowledge layer so AI can help with specs, prototypes, and design systems—without you repeating yourself every time.

title: ESPN's migration playbook: the move to Figma with One North
speakers: Elliott Muñoz (Creative Director, ESPN Creative Studio), Matt Rogers (Product Design Lead, One North)
abstract: Each game day broadcast features a host of slick visuals—digital scoreboards, player stats, and matchup graphics. But behind the scenes, each individual design requires a huge lift to get from concept to live on air. In this session, One North and ESPN will share how migrating from Photoshop to Figma revealed new ways to collaborate, scale, and accelerate workflows. From initial adoption to transformative impact, this talk highlights not only how Figma helped push ESPN's design system to the next level, but how their design system pushed One North to up our game, too.

title: Prototypes, failures, and unexpected breakthroughs
speakers: Noah Finer (Software Engineer, Figma)
abstract: Noah spends many days at Figma prototyping janky interactions, and other days perfecting editor microinteractions so subtle they almost go unnoticed. How do these two engineering mindsets interact? And how have they shaped the way Noah builds experiences like Figma Slides, FigPals, and interactive transit personality tests?

title: Build like it's 2030. Listen like you mean it.
speakers: Ned Dwyer (CEO & Co-founder, Great Question)

title: Reimagining NASA.gov to tell Earth’s most important stories
speakers: Ben Shown (Head of Design, Blink UX), Megan Greco (Senior Design Lead, Greenlight)
abstract: NASA’s digital universe just got a major upgrade. With a bold new web experience and the Horizon Design System, NASA is now equipped to tell its most important stories—breakthroughs, missions, and cosmic wonders—with clarity, cohesion, and style. In this session, Ben and Megan pull back the curtain on designing for rocket scientists, sharing how vision meets velocity in a high-stakes collaboration. Expect real talk on aligning stakeholders, designing for scale, and building a system that inspires the world—one pixel at a time.

title: Closing keynote
speakers: Patrick Flaherty (Senior Motion Designer, Microsoft), Tommy Geoco (Storyteller, UX Tools   ), Catt Small (Staff Product Designer, Dropbox), Matt D. Smith (CEO, Shift Nudge), Rasmus Andersson (Software Painter, Playbit)

title: Opening keynotes
speakers: Grant Sanderson (YouTube educator, 3Blue1Brown), Switch Angel + LO.FI.SCI.FI (Artists & Software Engineers)

title: Figma learning lab: upskill on skills and agentic workflows
speakers: Laura Fehre (Designer Advocate, Figma ), Manish Kumar (Developer Advocate, Figma)
abstract: Skills are how AI learns a domain: your research frameworks, handoff standards, and definitions of done, so every session starts informed, not from scratch. This session goes beyond the basics to show how to design, author, and deploy skills that make agents measurably faster and more precise for the work at hand. You’ll leave with a skill you helped build, not just a framework to complete later. Join us afterward in the Learning Lab Lounge for informal time with the Figma team.

title: Designing in code
speakers: Elle Shwer (Product Manager, Figma), Iris Lin (Product Designer, Figma), Nikolas Klein (Product Manager, Figma), Ben Chen (Engineering Manager, Figma)
abstract: The gap between designing and working in code is closing. Learn how two new launches bring code natively into Figma. Use Make to design in your local codebase, and Code layers to explore right on the canvas. Iterate, prototype, and collaborate in code all the way to production.

title: Designing in the age of the 10x engineer
speakers: Andy Zhang (Design Engineer Lead , Google)

title: 10 years of daily sketching
speakers: Zach Lieberman (Artist, MIT Media Lab)
abstract: Ten years ago, after a rough year, I posted a small code sketch on Instagram on New Year's Day and I've posted one every day since. This talk is about what I've learned from that practice of making something every day. I'll focus on the importance of boredom, how to structure iteration, how I structure research and most importantly, finding creative freedom.

title: The invisible mechanisms in your interfaces
speakers: Johannes Mutter (Design Engineer, mutter.co)

title: The world of street art and care-filled design
speakers: Ella Rochelle-Lawton (Project & Program Manager, First Amendment)

title: Welcome and opening remarks
speakers: Shaunt Voskanian (Chief Revenue Officer, Figma), Viral Shah (VP, Research, Figma)
abstract: Welcome and opening remarks

title: Dense by design
speakers: Matthew Ström-Awn (Founder,  mu.design)

title: Building real-time user insights into your design workflow
speakers: Prayag Narula (CEO & Co-Founder , HeyMarvin)

title: Building AI products at enterprise scale
speakers: Dylan Field (CEO & Co-founder, Figma), Jeetu Patel (President and Chief Product Officer, Cisco)

title: Managing multiple creative passions in a world that tells you to specialize
speakers: Lauren Hom (Lettering Artist & Designer, Hom Sweet Hom)
abstract: Lauren will share how she’s managed multiple creative passions, throughout her 15-year career, in a world that tells you to specialize.

title: Usernames on Instagram: how a craft fix became an identity crisis
speakers: Rose McManus (Content Designer, Instagram (Meta))

title: AI will upend your process—for the better
speakers: Justin Meyer (CEO, Bitovi)

title: Designing at the pace of model drops
speakers: Rodrigo Lemes (Ux Principal and OPS director, Mercado Livre), Paige Costello (VP Product, Figma)
abstract: Mercado Libre has built a culture where people take risks, make mistakes, and try again—even with over 1,500 designers inside a 30,000-person product, design, and engineering org spread over many countries, languages, and cultures. And as AI reshapes the tools almost weekly, the stakes only get higher. Principal Designer Rodrigo Lemes joins Figma VP Product Paige Costello to talk through how Mercado Libre turns its values into everyday practice and explore what it takes to keep a big org curious, autonomous, and willing to be wrong.

title: How Codex is rewriting the product playbook
speakers: Alexander Embiricos (Head of Enterprise, OpenAI), Kris Rasmussen (Chief Technology Officer, Figma)
abstract: In this fireside chat, Alex Embiricos, head of product for Codex at OpenAI, joins us to share the vision behind one of the most influential AI products in the market today. From early product decisions to real-world deployment, Alex will discuss how Codex evolved, what it reveals about the future of building, and how AI is transforming team workflows across product, design, and engineering.

title: Figma learning lab: express yourself with Figma Draw
speakers: Laura Butvinik (Manager, Education Content Strategy, Figma), Rachel Platt (Education Designer, Figma), Kristen Bates (Education Content Strategist, Figma)
abstract: A hands-on collaborative session where attendees come together to create a shared art piece. This session celebrates the magic of expressive design and showcases what’s possible with Figma Draw. Bring your laptop for the session and join us afterward in the Learning Lab Lounge for informal time with the Figma team.

title: The future is low-tech: lessons from the early 2000s
speakers: Jésabel DC (Tech Anthropologist & EdTech Founder, The Lifestyle Lab )

title: Inside the Weave workflow
speakers: Gal Sharir (Product Designer, Figma), Ron Baranov (Creative Workflow Advocate, Figma)
abstract: Dive into how Weave creative work actually gets made—not the polished final frame, but the decisions behind it. Explore branching, sequencing, iteration, model chaining, edits, mistakes, recoveries, and the systems thinking that turn AI from a magic trick into a real creative process.

title: The hidden craft of interface design in modern film
speakers: Jayse Hansen (Fictional UI Design Director, ØFFGRID)
abstract: Step behind the screens of modern cinema. Jayse Hansen takes you from sketchbook to IMAX, revealing the story-driven way holograms, HUDs, and futuristic interface systems are designed, animated, and brought to life for blockbuster films. Spanning more than 20 major franchises, from Iron Man and The Hunger Games to Tron and Star Wars, this is a rare backstage look at the meticulously detailed craft of designing technology that doesn’t exist, but feels like it should.

title: Figma learning lab: workflow tips—from slow to flow
speakers: Chad Bergman (Designer Advocate, Figma), Michel Ferreira (Designer Advocate, Figma)
abstract: In this session we’ll share a collection of helpful tips and workflow tricks that can help you work smarter, and faster on the canvas and beyond. Join us afterward in the Learning Lab Lounge for informal time with the Figma team.

title: Quirk up your workplace
speakers: Darby Thomas (Senior Product Designer, GitHub)
abstract: This talk is about encouraging people to be a little more silly, a little more playful when you’re at work. The rituals, gimmicks, and stunts we’ve done at GitHub to make remote work fun and interesting.

title: The canvas is not dead
speakers: Catt Small (Staff Product Designer, Dropbox)

title: Everyone's still eating: building Notion's design system
speakers: Tamara Chu (Design Engineer, Notion), Shauna Sapper (Software Engineer, Notion)

title: In defense of radical naivete
speakers: Archie Lee Coates IV (Co-Founder, PLAYLAB, INC.), Damien Correll (VP, Brand Studio, Figma)
abstract: Damien Correll, VP Brand Design & Creative at Figma, sits down with Archie Lee Coates IV, co-founder of PlayLab, to explore the creative power of radical naivete—the practice of questioning assumptions, challenging conventions, and approaching familiar problems with fresh eyes. Drawing on PlayLab’s work across brands, institutions, and cultural experiences, they’ll discuss how crossing disciplines and starting from first principles can lead to unexpected breakthroughs—especially as so many leaders today are navigating new problems and paradigms.

title: How design makes change legible
speakers: Collin Whitehead (VP of Design , Superhuman)
abstract: As many of us launch agents, products, and features at an unprecedented pace, maintaining clarity—for teams and users alike—becomes increasingly difficult. Drawing on lessons from scaling Superhuman and integrating new products into the business, Collin will share why design is uniquely positioned to create coherence across brands, teams, and experiences—and how design leaders can expand their role from managing craft to shaping strategy.

title: In praise of dark mode
speakers: Mehmet Aydın Baytaş (Senior Design Engineer, Attio)
abstract: Good design emits as little light as possible. This talk makes the case for Dark Mode Maximalism—that all user interfaces should be designed in dark mode. Drawing on two decades of design/engineering practice and arcane scholarship, Baytaş explains that dark mode is not a toggle, but the default condition of good design.

title: Preparing Rocket for the AI era
speakers: Emily Strobl (Director, Product Design - AI & Design Systems, Rocket), Will Hobick (Design Engineering Lead, Rocket)

title: Designing with confidence in an AI-powered world
speakers: Bobby Meixner (VP, Solution Marketing, UserTesting)

title: The next era of product building: Designing the workflows of the future
speakers: Yuhki Yamashita (Chief Product Officer, Figma), Kris Rasmussen (Chief Technology Officer, Figma), Loredana Crisan (Chief Design Officer, Figma)
abstract: As AI reshapes how products are imagined, designed, and built, the workflows behind software creation are changing just as quickly. In this conversation, Figma CTO Kris Rasmussen, and CDO Loredana Crisan join CPO Yuhki Yamashita to discuss how teams are adapting to new ways of working, what remains essential as workflows evolve, and the next generation of product creation.

title: Designed to be read: making a system AI can actually use
speakers: Harvey Whiting (Product Designer, Meta)

title: Designing brand intelligence: from rules to expression
speakers: Nicole Martinez (Co-founder, Let's Jetty)
abstract: In an AI-powered design world, the designer’s role is fundamentally shifting. The real edge is no longer technical execution but how you think, decide, and design in a world changing faster than your design system. This talk reimagines brand guides as cognitive frameworks inspired by J Dilla, who reimagined rhythm and broke away from rigid quantization to create something more human, more fluid, more felt. Learn how to organize brands by cognition, build interactive frameworks in Figma, and evolve from makers to strategic directors.

title: Getting real output from Figma Make
speakers: Brett McMillin (Designer Advocate, Figma), Laura Fehre (Designer Advocate, Figma )

title: Digitizing the public sector
speakers: Sau Sheong Chang (DCE (Products) & CTO, GovTech), Marcel Weekes (VP, Product Engineering, Figma)
abstract: Drawing on his experience building government digital services, Sau Sheong Chang explores how design, technology, and systems thinking come together to modernize institutions—and what private-sector leaders can learn from designing for everyone.

title: Applied Insights: shifting roles in product building
speakers: Matt Walker (Researcher, Figma), Mike Blight (Manager, Research, Figma)
abstract: In this interactive session, the Figma research team will explore how responsibilities across design, product, and engineering are shifting, and what that means for how teams work now.

title: Figma learning lab: build your first Weave workflow
speakers: Moran Dankner (Creative workflow advocate, Figma), Ken Vreman (Senior Design Advocate, Figma)
abstract: Node-based tools look technical until you build something with them. In this hands-on session, you'll go from blank canvas to working workflow, connecting models, directing outputs, and seeing exactly how Figma Weave gives you control over every step. Bring your laptop for the session and join us afterward in the Learning Lab Lounge for informal time with the Figma team.

title: Own the loop: how designers and researchers win with AI evals
speakers: Setor Zilevu (Researcher, Figma)
abstract: AI is everywhere, but how do we know if it's actually good? The teams winning with AI aren't just moving fast, they're measuring well. And that measurement starts with a human-centered definition of "good" that lives in design and research. AI evals have long been an engineering problem. That's changing. As capabilities commoditize, design is the new moat. This talk is an invitation for designers and researchers to lead and become architects of how we evaluate AI against real human needs.

title: Gnarly by design: building systems with grit
speakers: Beau Wingfield (Visual Product Designer, ex-EA, Full Circle Studio (skate.), Publix)
abstract: Clean design systems are hard to create, but gritty, texture-heavy design systems are even harder. Join Beau for a journey into a video game design system and learn how to add some character to your squeaky-clean system and make it scaleable.

title: 300GB story: how to inspire action when data isn’t enough
speakers: Luke Fiorante (Design Engineer, Harvard University), Sirinda Limsong (Product Designer, Harvard University)
abstract: More info coming soon...

title: Design context for agentic workflows
speakers: Jake Albaugh (Developer Advocate, Figma)

title: Design for the future of apps
speakers: Craig Labenz (Developer Relations Engineer, Google)
abstract: Generative AI is not only about text chats and image generation. At Google, AI is also increasingly used to compose ephemeral UI on demand, a pattern called Generative UI. While this promises unparalleled personalization for each user, it also risks regressions in UX pillars like consistency and clear information hierarchy. Learn how designers can adapt design systems to realize the full potential GenUI while also mitigating its risks.

title: Designing for agents: rethinking the role of the interface
speakers: Rachel Been (SVP of Design, Expedia), Charlie Sutton (Chief Design Officer, Atlassian), Yuhki Yamashita (Chief Product Officer, Figma)
abstract: As AI agents become more common, design is evolving beyond traditional interfaces toward systems that act on users’ behalf. This panel brings together design leaders from both B2B and consumer companies to share perspectives on emerging patterns, challenges, and opportunities in designing agent-driven experiences—and what it takes to build products that people understand and feel confident using.

title: The marathon designer: creative endurance beyond prompts and pixels
speakers: Socrates Charisis (Founder & Designer, Quintessential)
abstract: In a world obsessed with speed & chaos, design’s biggest threat isn’t AI—it’s burnout, pixel-pushing, doing things the old way, waiting for permission. This talk introduces a framework for creative endurance beyond prompts and pixels. Through three core practices—sustainable pacing, builder culture, and versatile hiring—Socrates Charisis shares lessons from running a bootstrapped studio in Athens. Expect real stories, actionable systems, and new ways to keep creativity alive when the work gets dull and the tools keep changing.

title: Don't forget the feeling: a critique of software in 2026
speakers: Luis Ouriach (Designer Advocate, Figma)
abstract: More info coming soon...

title: Designing money: how to build a trustworthy brand in the age of AI
speakers: Chris Warner (Creative Lead, Brand Studio, Plaid)
abstract: How do we pair machine learning with true artistry? What’s moral, what’s safe, and what genuinely resonates in this new creative world? These are the questions that confronted our team at Plaid as we sought to reshape the way money looks and feels in the age of AI. In this far-reaching talk, we’ll explore the ways authenticity has shaped the history of finance through the lens of graphic design and branding—and what machine learning means for its future.

title: Design systems anarchy
speakers: Lauren LoPrete (Head of Design Foundations, Mercury)
abstract: Most people don't get into design systems to be a cop. But we've become exactly that. Product teams go around us while we create rules nobody follows. Punk figured this out decades ago. You need shared ethos, basic infrastructure, and people who care. Quality comes from culture, not compliance. Drawing from years running an indie record label and nearly 10 years leading systems at scale at Block, Dropbox, and Expedia, this talk applies punk's blueprint to systems. AI handed designers the means of production. The gatekeeping model is dead. Stop managing systems. Start nurturing subcultures.

title: Legibility by design
speakers: Ryan Powell (Head of Design, Waymo)
abstract: Building trust in AI isn't about transparency; it’s about communication design. Whether it’s an autonomous vehicle or a generative agent, the challenge is the same: how much should a system communicate? Drawing from years at Waymo, this session explores a framework for trust. We’ll move beyond automation toward legibility, where users understand intent rather than just monitoring tasks. Learn to calibrate feedback to avoid alert fatigue and build AI products that feel predictable and fundamentally trustworthy.

title: Closing keynote
speakers: Brent David Freaney (Art Director, Special Offer, Inc.), André Anjos (Artist , RAC)
abstract: From the process behind a single shade of green for Charli XCX's Brat to the systems thinking that holds together large-scale creative campaigns, Brent will share how he approaches design work that spans albums, magazines, live media, and screens. He'll walk through his process, his tools, and what it actually takes to design for cultural impact.
